channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Oscar-winning actor Gene Hackman, wife found dead and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU pushes back against Trump tariff threats and his caustic comments that bloc is out to get the US
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The European Union on Thursday pushed back hard against allegations by U.S. President Donald Trump that the 27-nation bloc was out to get the United States, and warned that it would vigorously fight any wholesale tariff of 25% on all EU products.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pope continues slow recovery from double pneumonia
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oscar-winner Gene Hackman, his wife and their dog found dead in their New Mexico home
item.description: The gruff-but-beloved Hackman was among the finest actors of his generation, appearing as both villains, heroes and antiheroes in dozens of dramas, comedies and action films from the 1960s until his retirement in the early 2000s.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nurses' stories recount terror of armed man's attack at Pennsylvania hospital
item.description: The stories of two nurses who survived a gunman's attack on an intensive care unit in a York, Pennsylvania, hospital are being shared in Facebook posts that reveal new details.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Utah is one step away from becoming 1st state to ban fluoride in water
item.description: The Utah governor's signature is all that stands between a state ban on water fluoridation after House Bill 81 cleared its final hurdle in the legislature on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Drachman, WGN 9

item.title: New details released in Lake Oswego shooting that damaged SAIF CEO's home
item.description: Authorities are seeking information about a suspect believed to have fired multiple rounds into the home of the CEO of an Oregon workers' compensation insurance organization, causing damage to the front door and minor damage to the home.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Plante, NewsNation

item.title: Texas lottery drawings that paid out big jackpots are the focus of widening investigatons
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Two major lottery drawings in Texas that put nearly $180 million in the pockets of winning ticket holders have set off widening state investigations over concern that ticket sellers and buyers may have exploited the rules. The Texas Lottery, one of the largest in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bodycam video shows arrest of 4 suspects in FedEx driver robbery
item.description: In the bodycam video, two are seen being apprehended by police, before an officer chases down and handcuffs the final suspect shortly after.
item.dc:creator: Jusolyn Flower, NewsNation

item.title: Bloody crime scene photos play key role in trial of man accused in Palestinian American boy's death
item.description: JOLIET, Ill. (AP) — Photos of a child's bloody body, a black knife holder and other crime scene evidence took center stage Wednesday at the murder and hate crime trial for an Illinois landlord accused of fatally stabbing a 6-year-old Palestinian American boy in 2023.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Super Bowl sports reporter found dead in hotel had sedative in his system: authorities
item.description: The woman accused of taking the reporter's phone and bank cards has previously been accused of drugging men to steal from them, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, KRON4

item.title: Trump: ‘Concession Agreement’ with Venezuela will be terminated
item.description: In his post, Trump slammed the “Electoral conditions” within Venezuela and argued Maduro’s regime failed to meet the concessions given by Biden.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Kentucky woman accused of marrying her biological father
item.description: A paternity test claimed the probability that Kimberly Tempel and Bradly McCollom were father and daughter were greater than 99.99%
item.dc:creator: Zach McKnight, NewsNation

item.title: Mystery illnesses claim over 50 lives in Congo: What we know
item.description: Unidentified illnesses in northwestern Congo have killed more than 50 people over the past five weeks, nearly half of them within hours after they felt sick.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge criticizes Justice Department's broad reading of Trump's Capitol riot pardons
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday criticized the Justice Department's evolving position that a presidential pardon for a Kentucky man who stormed the Capitol also covers his conviction for illegally possessing guns at his home. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democratic governors pitch state jobs to federal workers cut by Trump
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Some of America's governors — mostly Democrats — have a message for the wave of fired federal workers: We want you.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police killings set record in 2024, but with smallest increase in years: Report
item.description: In 2024, police officers in the US killed 1,365 people, a slight increase from the 1,329 civilians killed in 2023, according to the Mapping Police Violence report.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: American Airlines flight discontinues landing hours before similar incident with Southwest
item.description: The flight coming from Boston had a similar landing procedure to a Southwest Airlines flight in Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Christianity in US stops decline after years of losses: Survey
item.description: Although down substantially from 2007, 44 percent of Americans said they pray at least once a day, a figure that has remained consistent in the last four years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to rule for Ohio woman claiming job bias because she's straight
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court seemed likely Wednesday to side with an Ohio woman who claims she suffered sex discrimination from her employer because she is straight.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NFLPA report cards: Dolphins, Vikings finish at the top for second straight year
item.description: The Miami Dolphins are the top-ranked team, followed by the Minnesota Vikings, for the second consecutive season in the NFL Players Association report card.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Recent aviation disasters and close calls stoke fears about the safety of flying
item.description: All the recent aviation disasters and close calls have people worried about the safety of flying. The midair collision that killed 67 near Washington D.C. last month was the worst disaster.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Michelle Trachtenberg, 'Gossip Girl' and 'Buffy' actress, dead at 39
item.description: Actress Michelle Trachtenberg has died, police sources confirmed to multiple news outlets on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Child becomes first measles death reported in Texas outbreak
item.description: An unvaccinated school-aged child who was hospitalized with measles has passed away, marking the first death in the outbreak, according to Lubbock and state health officials.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Rooney, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pitches 'gold card' as replacement for EB5 investor visa
item.description: The Trump administration will be revamping the U.S. investor visa, increasing the funds required to secure the status while dubbing the new option a "gold card.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US and Ukraine near an economic deal with mineral rights but no security promise, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine and the U.S. have reached an agreement on a framework for a broad economic deal that would include access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals, three senior Ukrainian officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, HANNA ARHIROVA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Palm Beach, Florida hospital attack 'shouldn't have happened:' Nurse's daughter
item.description: "The fact that this happened at a healthcare facility is beyond me," Dr. Cindy Joseph said on "Banfield.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Judge OKs prison abuse settlement, rejecting Trump administration's push to rewrite protections
item.description: A judge on Tuesday gave final approval to a legal settlement that subjects the federal Bureau of Prisons to independent monitoring and increased transparency in the wake of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual abuse at a now-shuttered federal women’s prison in California. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Another $500M for Hurricane Helene relief in North Carolina passes key hurdle
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Another $500 million would be spent toward Hurricane Helene recovery efforts in western North Carolina in legislation the state House unanimously approved Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Millions of Social Security recipients are about to get back payments
item.description: More than 3.2 million Social Security recipients who received pensions from their time as teachers, firefighters, police officers and other public service jobs will soon see a boost in their benefits.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Wu-Tang Clan announces Wu-Tang Forever: The Final Chamber Tour
item.description: The Wu-Tang Clan is hitting the road for the final time. The iconic East Coast hip-hop group announced their farewell tour called Wu-Tang Forever: The Final Chamber.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Musk extends deadline to describe work
item.description: More than two million federal workers remain uncertain about their job security after last night's deadline to respond to an email from Elon Musk, the man President Donald Trump has tapped to make the government more efficient.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, WGN 9

item.title: Police: Holland mom said she killed son to fulfill his death wish
item.description: A mom accused of stabbing her son to death told police she was helping him fulfill his wish: to die before his 18th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek facing a deadline from Elon Musk to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4
